CCP4Table.title: Mn(I/sigI) and CC(1/2) [in P1] vs. resolution

plot.title: Resolution estimate 1.07A
plot.description: Plot of data strength vs. resolution, and cutoffs to keep only strong data for analysis CC(1/2) [in P1] (threshold 0.60), or <I/sigma(I)> (threshold 6.00) High resolution limit reset to 1.07 using Mn(I/sigmaI) cutoff

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CC(1/2) N_CC CCfit Mn(I/sigI) N (I/sigI)/10

POINTLESS.CutoffMessage: High resolution limit reset to 1.07 using Mn(I/sigmaI) cutoff

CCP4Table.title: Axial reflections, axis a (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis a, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis a, screw axis 2(1)

CCP4Table.title: Axial reflections, axis b (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis b, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis b, screw axis 2(1)

CCP4Table.title: Axial reflections, axis c (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis c, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis c, screw axis 2(1)

POINTLESS.SolutionMessage: Choosing specified space group P 21 21 21 with reindex operator [h,k,l]

AIMLESS.BatchGroupMessage: Analysis by Batch is in groups of batches, group width 1.00 degrees, 1 batches/group

CCP4Table.title: === Scales v rotation range, ds1

plot.title: Mn(k) & 0k (theta=0) v. batch
plot.description: Mn(k) (red) is the mean scale over the whole resolution range for each Batch. 0k is the scale at the lowest resolution, ie excluding the relative B-factor

plot.title: Relative Bfactor & Decay v. batch
plot.description: The relative B-factor is largely a radiation damage correction. Negative values below perhaps -10 may indicate sever radiation damage. Bdecay is a straight-line fit to the B-factors

CCP4Table.headers: N Run Phi Batch Mn(k) 0k Number Bfactor Bdecay

CCP4Table.title: Analysis against all Batches for all runs, ds1

plot.title: Rmerge v Batch for all runs
plot.description: Increase of Rmerge towards the end of a run probably indicates radiation damage. The red line is smoothed over adjacent batches

plot.title: Filtered Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Batch
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be ~1.0 over all ranges

plot.title: Mean(Chi^2), Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Batch
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be ~1.0 over all ranges

plot.title: Cumulative %completeness & Anom%cmpl v Batch
plot.description: In conjunction with radiation damage indicators, cumulative completeness may indicate a suitable point for cutting back poor data. The blue line is the completeness of anomalous differences

plot.title: Maximum resolution limit, I/sigma > 1.0
plot.description: The resolution limit estimate is the point at which I/sig(I) falls below 1.0, red line smoothed over adjacent batches. A sharp increase probably indicates radiation damage

plot.title: Imean & RMS Scatter
plot.description: Major deviations may indicate bad images

plot.title: Imean/RMS scatter
plot.description: Major deviations may indicate bad images

CCP4Table.headers: N Batch Mn(I) RMSdev I/rms Rmerge Number Nrej Cm%poss AnoCmp MaxRes CMlplc Chi^2 Chi^2c SmRmerge SmMaxRes

CCP4Table.title: Correlations CC(1/2) within dataset, ds1

plot.title: CC(1/2) v resolution, max resolution 0.00, anom 0.00
plot.description: Resolution estimate: Insufficient data to determine resolution limit Anomalous resolution: Insufficient data to determine resolution limit

plot.title: RMS anomalous correlation ratio
plot.description: RMS correlation ratio > 1.0 indicates significant anomalous differences

plot.title: Rsplit
plot.description: Rsplit is the R-factor equivalent of CC(1/2), but less useful

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CCanom Nanom RCRanom CC1/2 NCC1/2 CC1/2v Rsplit

CCP4Table.title: Anisotropy analysis of CC(1/2) and I/sd, ds1

plot.title: Anisotropic CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) in cones along principle directions. Estimated maximum resolution limits, a*: 0.00, b*: 0.00, c*: 0.00

plot.title: Anisotropic Mn(I/sd) v resolution
plot.description: Mn(I/sd) in cones along principle directions

plot.title: Projected CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) projected on principle directions

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CC:a* CC:b* CC:c* I/sd:a* I/sd:b* I/sd:c* CCp1 CCp2 CCp3

CCP4Table.title: Cumulative CC(1/2) in resolution ranges vs. Batch

plot.title: Cumulative CC(1/2)
plot.description: Cumulative CC(1/2) in coarse resolution ranges

CCP4Table.title: Analysis against resolution, ds1

plot.title: I/sigma, Mean Mn(I)/sd(Mn(I))
plot.description: I/sigma = I/RMS = I/(rms scatter before merging). Mean(I/sd) after averaging, ~= signal/noise

plot.title: Filtered Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Mean(Chi^2), Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Rmerge, Rfull, Rmeas, Rpim v Resolution
plot.description: Rmerge, classic R-factor; Rmeas, multiplicity-weighted R; Rpim, precision-indicating R

plot.title: Average I, RMSdeviation and Sd
plot.description: RMSdev is RMS scatter, sd is average corrected sig(I) estimate

plot.title: Fractional bias
plot.description: FrcBias = Mean( Mn(If) - Ip )/Mean( Mn(I) ), where If is a full if present, else the partials with the smallest number of parts

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Rmrg Rfull Rcum Rmeas Rpim Nmeas AvI RMSdev sd I/RMS Mn(I/sd) FrcBias Chi^2 Chi^2c

CCP4Table.title: Analysis against resolution, with & without anomalous (Ov), ds1

plot.title: Rmerge, Rmeas, Rpim v Resolution
plot.description: within I+/I- sets, and over all data (Ov values)

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Rmrg RmrgOv Rcum RcumOv Rmeas RmeasOv Rpim RpimOv Nmeas

CCP4Table.title: Analysis against intensity, ds1

plot.title: Rmerge v Intensity
plot.description: The important values are in the top bin: Rmerge: 0.000 Rmeas: 0.000 Rpim: 0.000

CCP4Table.headers: Imax Rmrg Rcum Rmeas Rpim Nmeas AvI RMSdev sd I/RMS Mn(I/sd) FrcBias

CCP4Table.title: Completeness & multiplicity v. resolution, ds1

plot.title: Completeness v Resolution
plot.description: %poss, completeness in shell; C%poss, cumulative completeness. Anomalous completeness (AnomCmpl) is the percentage of possible anomalous differences measured. AnomFrc is the % of measured acentric reflections for which an anomalous difference has been measured

plot.title: Multiplicity v Resolution
plot.description: Total multiplicity and anomalous multiplicity

CCP4Table.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Nmeas Nref Ncent %poss C%poss Mlplct AnoCmp AnoFrc AnoMlt

AIMLESS.AnomalousStatus: The anomalous signal appears to be weak so anomalous flag was left OFF

CCP4Table.title: Weighted second moments after Anisotropy Correction

plot.title: Weighted second moments vs resolution (acentric only)

InformationContent.Text:
Estimate of information depends on: accuracy of experimental sigmas accuracy of anisotropy and tNCS parameters status of twinning (which reduces information)
Estimated total information = 32918 bits, or 22817 nats in 10639 reflections average of 3.09409 bits per reflection Number of reflections exceeding information threshold: 0.01 bit: 10639 0.05 bit: 10638 0.1 bit: 10637 0.5 bit: 10384 1 bit: 9477 2 bits: 7103

Truncation.Warning: FLAT prior in use due to either tNCS or twinning. To override force --prior WILSON.

DataStatistics.Comment: The Completeness max and min resolution indicte the resolution extremes where obs/sig(obs) > N accounts for more than 85 % of observations.
The Completeness with I/sigma above 3 indicates a strong signal, here it is from 8.94 to 1.10A. (The CC1/2 on unmerged intensities gives, perhaps, the best estimate of the useable data.)

• This will be used in the analysis of the data.
  • Strong data does not extend to low resolutions.
  • Processed data contains a lot of weak data at high resolution. Moments plots and Wilson plot should be consulted.

CCP4Table.title: intensity completeness vs resolution

plot.title: Resolution plots for completeness at various I/sigmaI levels
Resolution plots for completeness and Rstandard

CCP4Table.headers: 1/resol^2 completeness I/sigI>15 I/sigI>10 I/sigI>5 I/sigI>3 I/sigI>2 I/sigI>1 I/sigI>1 Rstandard

DataStatistics.Comment: The completeness at various resolution limit plots gives the completeness after applying a I/sigI cutoff. The profiles give an indication of the quality of the data. The Rstandard plot (<sigF>/ <F>) gives an alternative indicator. Strongly recorded resolution bins would typically have values below 0.1

Low completeness at low resolution can lead to map distortions and other difficulties. This often arises through experimental effects such as incorrectly alligned crystal, poorly positioned backstop, or over exposure.

IceRingsAnalysis.Comment: The ice rings table shows data Z-scores and completeness for ice ring sensitive resolutions in comparison with neighbouring ice-ring insensitive resolutions. Large z-scores and low completeness at give a strong hint to the presence of ice rings. It may be required to exclude these resolution ranges.

DataStatistics.Comment: The isotropic Wilson temperature estimate (B-value) is an approximation to the fall-off of scattering with resolution. This should be correlated with the refined atomic B-values. This averages out any anisotropy in the experimental observations. This approximation will be misleading for strongly anisotropic data.

The wilson plot shows the fall off of the mean intensity with resolution. This is then used calculate an absolute scale and temperature factor for a set of observed intensities, using the theory of A C Wilson. The reference_plot is based upon an analysis of high resolution datasets in the PDB (BEST or RNA equivalent), which takes into account the none random distribution of atoms within the crystal. Some deviation from the reference plot is to be expected, however, significant deviation may indicate problems, such as ice rings, detector issues, or missprocessed.
DataStatistics.Reference: Popov and Bourenkov, Acta D (2003) D59, 1145

OutlierRingsAnalysis.Comment: The outlier rings table shows data Z-scores and completeness for problem resolution bins in comparison with the Wilson B-factor fit. Large z-scores and low completeness at give a strong hint to the presence of problems. It may be required to exclude these resolution ranges.

AnomStatistics.Comment:
These calculations are performed using scaled and merged data. More accurate estimates of the limit of the anomalous signal can be obtained using scaled and unmerged data in the half dataset correlation calculation of aimless.
The measurability is defined as the fraction of the anomalous differences for which the signal to noise for deltaI, I(+) and I(-) are all > 3. The resolution limits are derived from I/sigI of 1.2 for the signal to noise, and measurability above 5% For well processed data having more than 5% of the measured data satisfying this criteria is a good indicator, particularly when combined with a significate mean signal to noise of the anomalous difference in a resolution shell.

plot.title: Resolution plots for anomalous scattering indicators

translationalNCS.Comment: Translational NCS has been detected at ( 0,0,0.5) .The probability that this is by chance is 0.67 This will have a major impact on the twinning estimates and effectiveness of the truncate procedure.

The analysis uses the peak heights in the patterson map that are further than 14 A (approx. 4 Ca-Ca) from the origin. The presence of a large off origin peak (above 20%) and/or a very low Q-score, below 1.0, is a string indicator of the presence of tNCS. An intermidiate Q-score, between 5.0 and 1.0, may indicate weak tNCS or be the result of cross vector of a large scatterer such as a cluster or heavy metal.

AnisotropyAnalysis.Comment: Little or no anisotropy detected. The presence of anisotropy may indicate that the crystal is poorly ordered along one of the axes.

CCP4Table.title: Intensities Anisotropy Analysis

plot.title: Directorional plots for number of reflections

CCP4Table.headers: 1/resol^2 Mn(I(d1)) Mn(I(d2)) Mn(I(d3)) Mn(I(ov) Mn(I/sd(d1)) Mn(I/sd(d2)) Mn(I/sd(d3)) Mn(I/sd(ov)) N(d1) N(d2) N(d3) N

AnisotropyAnalysis.Comment: The directional plots are along the directions of the moments of the anisotropy temperature matrix. These are ordered such that d1 has maximum alignment with a*, d2 with b*, etc.

Twinning.Comment: The appearance of twinning operators only indicates that the crystal symmetry and lattice symmetry permit twinning. It does not mean that there is twinning present. Only the presence of statistics consistent with twinning gives a strong indicator.

L-test.Comment: Relation between L statistics and twinning fraction: Twinning fraction = 0.000 L statistics = 0.500: Twinning fraction = 0.100 L statistics = 0.440: Twinning fraction = 0.500 L statistics = 0.375:

Moments.Comment: The acentric moments plot can also give an indication of the quality of the high resolution data. Large deviations often indicate bias in the data.

Twinning.Comment: These tests rely on the fact that it is highly improbably that very weak or very strong reflections will coincide, therefore, the tails for the distribution of twinned datasets will be less pronounced

CCP4Table.title: Cumulative intensity distribution
CCP4Table.headers: Z Acent_theor Acent_twin Acent_obser Cent_theor Cent_obser

Twinning.Comment: The culmulative intensity, N(Z), plot is diagnostic for both twinning and tNCS. For twinned data there are fewer weak reflections, therefore, N(Z) is sigmoidal for twinned data. However, if both twinning and tNCS are present, the effects may cancel each out. Therefore the results of the L-test and patterson test should be consulted

plot.title: cumulative distribution function for |L|, twin fraction of 0.03

Twinning.Comment: The Cumulative |L| plot for acentric data, where L = (I1-I2)/(I1+I2). This depends on the local difference in intensities. The difference operators used link to the neighbouring reflections taking into account possible tNCS operators. Note that this estimate is not as reliable as obtained via the H-test or ML Britton test if twin laws are available. However, it is less prone to the effects of anisotropy than the H-test.
Twinning.Reference: Reference: Padilla, Yeates. A statistic for local intensity differences: robustness to anisotropy and pseudo-centering and utility for detecting twinning. Acta Cryst. D59, 1124-30, 2003
